item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely one of my favourite articles in the site

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image is owned by The School of Engineering and Applied Sciences of Miami University, Oxford, OH, and is under All Rights Reserved. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, huge necropost but this reminds me of a quote Nikola Tesla himself wrote shortly before his death: "You will live to see man-made horrors beyond your comprehension."
Yep, really good article, nice Job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: also it was meant to be an energy weapon not a bomb, there shouldn't be a fail safe inside it to prevent it from going off if taken apart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda put off by the fact there is no reason it couldn't have just been dumped into a universe with no life in it. Besides maybe the guys from that demention wanted ours destryoed and Tesla was too bummed out to think about that….. but evechaly before his death he would have been like "WAIT A MINUTE" …. i mean …. come on… x.x

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very big necropost but I don't see why Tessa CAN return to our universe but CAN'T send the bomb to an uninhabited one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems like the perfect SCP to throw into 1322 or 1437 or at a last resort 1162
yay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very interesting SCP and one of my personal favourites. I really like how it's led up to be thought of as something and then turns out to be something else, that is a really creative decision.

item.title: Re: Apollyon Reclassification?
item.content:encoded: I don't use Apollyon.

item.title: Re: Apollyon Reclassification?
item.content:encoded:
Personally, i don't think "Apollyon" Class should be a thing. At least not foundation-wide. Clearance level 5 and 6 internal? sure why not. Who knows how they (i.e. the O5) diversify between SCP objects where we only have 3 main Classes.

It's about morale here. What would be labeled as Apollyon are just Keter objects which need a revision on their containment protocol,that's all. When the options are between us finding a permanent containment or us facing an XK, YK or ZK scenario, then we will find it. We will Secure. We will Contain. We will Protect. No one else will. No one else can.

Giving those Keters a special label would be like admitting we are in over our heads with those. And that's an impression we must let spread among the agents and scientists doing their best and risking their lives.

I know that just sounded super melodramatic; and it is. But this is also part of udholding the Foundations corporate identity. it's something all organizaions do to raise motivation among employees/members.

just my 2 cents

item.title: Re: Apollyon Reclassification?
item.content:encoded:
Also, Sir, that was a nice draft you published; I think it would make a good SCP, just not a good SCP-3000; but that's aside the point.

Anyways, again, the author can do whatever he wants with this article, I was just saying that seeing as Apollyon is likely to become the new object class, that it would be wise (In my opinion, at least) to update it to Apollyon. Again, that's just a suggestion; the author can do whatever he damned pleases with this article.

item.title: Re: Apollyon Reclassification?
item.content:encoded:
(Apollyon = Abaddon) . Abaddon/Apollyon means that it could have a huge posibility wiping out human life. Apollyon classes objects tend to be uncontained and extremely dangerous.

You are generalizing from a tiny dataset, from which alternative conclusions are also possible.

Abaddon was named after a Hebrew God (I think) who caused destruction.

Jews are monotheists and pretty definitively so. Abaddon is a place in the Torah, and specifically is, from context, a (or the) realm of the dead (which is why in Christian goetics it becomes a principality of Hell). It means "destruction".

In Revelations Abaddon/Apollyon is an angel who commands the army of locusts in the eschaton.

item.title: Re: Apollyon Reclassification?
item.content:encoded: Are you sure? 2317 is still contained, but he will eventually break out; just as 2700 will eventually go to its ground energy state.

item.title: Re: Apollyon Reclassification?
item.content:encoded:
ianofdoom2

(Apollyon = Abaddon) . Abaddon/Apollyon means that it could have a huge posibility wiping out human life. Apollyon classes objects tend to be uncontained and extremely dangerous.

Abaddon was named after a Hebrew God (I think) who caused destruction.

item.title: Apollyon Reclassification?
item.content:encoded:
Hey,

Although I do not intend to speak on behalf of the author, neither do I intend on making any changes, seeing as the Apollyon object class might become an official object class, I was intrigued to know if this SCP would be reclassified as Apollyon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what if introducing this in to 682 causes it to become god in a reformed universe? Too risky, crosstesting rejected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been browsing for a number of years but made an account just to ask this question or ask someone to correct my understanding. Earlier in the page, you said that there are 7 habitable universes, to the traveling race's contemporary understanding. However, the implication is that there are many, many more which are permanently sans life as a result of the nature of those universes. If that's the case, why wouldn't the multi-universal think tank push the entropy device into one of those uninhabitable universes after they've finished harvesting materials from it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tesla fans usually antagonize Edison as they're rivals in their time. vice versa. fans usually accused one as stealing other's work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Skip = "SCP" pronounced like a word. (Community/very informal in-universe) nickname for SCP objects and entities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edison as the bad guy?

Am I out of the loop here?

I mean didn't he invent electricity or whatever?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep seeing the word "skip" being used in odd ways on this site. Mind explaining?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just have to say that him apologizing not because he brought an activated doomsday device back with him, but because he couldn't make this universe worth saving, is a genius move.

I'm still not happy that he feels that way, even though that's probably the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I spend my long hours before the pond with the birds. They are so blissfully unaware of what approaches, and that gives me just enough tranquility to tolerate my own existence.

…Duck pond reference?

item.title: Re: Grammar?
item.content:encoded: Anachronism did occur to me, and I actually looked that up specifically while writing this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-319, in all likelihood. I also drew upon 319 while writing 2995.

item.title: Re: Grammar?
item.content:encoded:
Seconding this. Also, was QWERTY not invented after Tesla's time?

EDIT: oops, sorry. No it wasn't.

item.title: Grammar?
item.content:encoded:
Should the phrase "SCP-2700 held at Secure Bunker-███" say "SCP-2700 is being held at Secure Bunker-███"?

Other than that, nice job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has the 2000 tag, which means it was entered in the 2000 contest that opened Series III. It's also fairly similar to another skip whose number I cannot rightly recollect at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't this used to have a different SCP number, under Series II?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't DISLIKE this SCP, but as noted above, it's a bomb built by scientists from the only 7 known LIFE BEARING universes. There's an obvious solution: dump it where it can't hurt anybody.
Not that the Foundation necessarily has that option.

A thought that tickles my fancy is that the diary could be a forgery. -2700 might be a real threat, or not, but the story with it's gaps comes from one journal from written by a dead man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay! One of the things I mentioned a while back was recently changed, and I like the new content!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1322? Haven't we done enough to them already?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… can't get into this, and I've just realised why. It isn't just because this is literally just a bomb, and a thing what does a thing and will accidentally ur whole universe. It's because this thing is not a threat to the Foundation on any level, and requires a massive dose of wilfull ignorance to make it seem spooky.

But it's very well written. So kudos there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome.

I hope for another SCP or story involving Tesla. He is such a attractive character for Foundation universe, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Present between within the frame is a continuous current of plasma, generated and perpetuated by unknown means.

"Between" or "within" needs to be removed there. I think "within" should remain because it wouldn't require changing the rest of the sentence, whereas "between" might require tweaking things a little more to sound right.

Attention, Clearance 4/2700 personnel: the fact that you are able to read this text verifies your security clearance and access to the following documentation. Please continue down to Document-SCP-2700-DE. Do not discuss this information with individuals lacking the appropriate clearance under any circumstances.

The bolded "to" needs to be added. The italicized portion does not need to be there. Security levels exist for a reason. 4s don't tell 1s, 2s, or 3s level 4 clearance information. That's a given. So it's pointlessly redundant to have that in there.

Actually… I suggest just taking that paragraph out and putting the whole secret bit into a collapsible that says something along the lines of, "Clearance 4/2700 Required. Enter Credentials" like I've seen on a few others with a "cover story" version. If you don't go that route, then I'd say do what I mentioned in the previous paragraph and just take out the part I italicized.

Not making any of the changes I mentioned here, as one is kinda minor (adding one word), but the others are a bit larger/enough that I think it'd be better for the author to make them rather than take a guess myself. So instead of fixing that one sentence now, I'll let them do that when they fix the other piece(s).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After seeing the comments about putting this SCP into SCP-093's world, something dreadful occurred to me: What if the universe traveling stranger in Tesla's story is the same as "Him" in SCP-093 that caused the current state of that locale?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why not dump it in the parallel universe on the other end of SCP-096 SCP-093?

E: Derp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic! I love the look at the intelligent man who wishes to "cull the herd" as it were. Nihilism and subterfuge. Always a fun time. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this one. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 319 is one of my absolute favorite articles, and I won't deny that the object in this one is based on that. I tried to emphasize Tesla's story, and how the theme of the object intertwines with his psyche to distinguish this article from Swann's. I don't begrudge the neutral vote, I just hope people see this article as revering Swann's, rather than ripping it off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Argh… I really like the sense of hopelessness this one portrays, since there's nothing we can do to stop the "Big Crunch", and I enjoyed reading Tesla's story, but this is too similar to SCP-319 for me to upvote. Neutral vote, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, what Caol said. "Gee, isn't that a bit… excessive?"
Then again, locked box test. Maybe this lockbox just needs to be camoflauged?

Anyway, I'm thinking that the Traveler reeeeeeally should have checked out some psychiatric evaluations first. Great minds obviously don't think alike.
And the Watcher can [DATA EXPUNGED] and die.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except they are completely different in term of effect. Simplifying either article to "apocalyptic science orb" does a discredit to both of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This and 319 are pretty much the exact same thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, happy birthday dude!
I enjoyed the read, but one point I want to put forward from my own opinion is that the personnel restrictions on the bunker were kind of a dead giveaway that the "safe" classification was misinformation. Granted, I didn't know right away what this actually was, but as soon as I read that restriction, my thought was, 'that's a lot of security for a safe'.
Now, I'm not suggesting you change your whole thing, but maybe add some kind of misinformation that keeps personnel from learning of the level 4 restriction.
Thanks, everyone…really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP reminds me a lot of SCP-319, because how they're both devices that (somehow) contains a scientific end-of-universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… morally ambiguous/evil misanthrope Tesla.

Welp. Here's my upvote. : I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've modified the ending of the article to read more like how I originally envisioned; I hope this works better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just what I was thinking. Either 093 or 1322.

On second thought, given what the residents of 1322 can do with physics…

+ Other idea
-Oh God

What if the Watcher realized that Tesla would likely sacrifice his own world to save the one in which the device was built? What if our world was his real target all along?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tesla again? If this wasn't so well written, I would have stayed with a "meh".

This is an odd milestone to note, but it's actually my first SCP article to breach +75 (though one is at +74 and I have a tale at +82). Three-fourths of the way to the big one hundred!

I don't really care about winning the contest, I knew who and what article deserved to take the gold from the start (that's not to say I didn't give them all a fair chance). I'm glad to have competed and gave it my all.

Thank you, everyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it would be very possible to take his name out and let the reader believe it's Tesla if they want to (for the aforementioned "who else is smart enough" reasons).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well my headcanon is that the Foundation keeps information on the really volatile XK (or YK in this case) objects very confidential, so it's done here as a sort of "nothing to see here" approach.

Secondly, I did it to play with the sort of archetype I've developed regarding Keter class articles. So it's something like "Wait, Anborough made a Safe article for his 2000 entry, what on Earth-wait it's Keter, I thought so-oh crap it's very, very Keter."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the fake-out is needed here. I like the story a lot, and I'm pleased to see that the Foundation is trying to go through the operating system, the most sensible route to disarm this. But the fake-out really bugs me. What does it add? All it is is the true version with a lot of information removed and a bunch of phony conjecture substituted. Neutral for now, but if you removed the fake-out, I'd probably upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I had the same reaction (Tesla? Really?), except that the Tesla in this article won me over. I wouldn't change it, I like it the way it is — but I can understand the argument for changing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe if there was something about having 507 dump it in another universe?
